exemplar.title: Should Kingalarm integrate ambulance services into its already existing security company?

detailedExemplarData.title: Should Kingalarm integrate ambulance services into its already existing security company?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept and sustains focus on it in title, introduction, tools, and conclusion
Strong analysis connecting KingAlarmâs resources and context to the concept of change
Consistent linkage of primary data from interviews to concept
weaknesses: Concept integration is uneven in some descriptive sections that lack explicit reference to change
A few analytical paragraphs could deepen the conceptual thread by tying back to change
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work in Band 4 for both understanding/integration and organizational connection. The student shows a strong grasp of âchangeâ and applies it throughout, but some descriptive sections remain purely factual without explicit conceptual linkage. A score of 4 reflects âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysisâ.

strengths: Provides four relevant supporting documents (Gleaner articles, competitor website, interview, visuals) meeting IB requirements
Documents are sufficiently in-depth to inform analysis
Well-organized listing and clear referencing of each document
weaknesses: Lacks financial or statistical data to broaden depth
Supporting documents are not annotated to highlight specific linkages to main analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student supplies the required 3â5 documents (Band 4 for number), the depth and range of ideas remain limited by lack of detailed data. The overall relevance is solid but not extensive, fitting Band 3: âdocuments are relevant and sufficiently in-depth.â Hence a score of 3.

strengths: Effective selection of three core strategic tools (Ansoff, SWOT, Force-field) well suited to the diversification question
Each tool is applied directly to KingAlarmâs context
Clear relevance to the research question
weaknesses: Application sometimes remains descriptive without deeper evaluation (e.g., unweighted SWOT factors)
Positioning within Ansoff matrix and force-field scoring criteria need more explicit detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection of tools is strong (Band 4), but application is uneven and at times merely descriptive (Band 3). The descriptor âadequate selection and application, relevance not always clearâ best matches Band 3, so a score of 3 is warranted.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses primary interview data and qualitative market information within tools
Analysis goes beyond description with competitor comparisons and force-field evaluation
Mostly effective reasoning showing integration across sections
weaknesses: Evidence base is small and narrative; lacks quantitative support (e.g., market projections)
Force-field scoring lacks defined scales, and threat assessments lack likelihood/impact weighting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is adequate (Band 3), while analysis quality is mostly effective with evidence of integration (Band 4). On balance, the work aligns with Band 4: âmostly effective analysis with some integration,â justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question with clear âyes, proceed with mitigationâ
Consistently draws on previous analyses and remains congruent with tool outcomes
weaknesses: Introduces new recommendations (subscription upsell) without prior analysis
Could better summarize major risks and tie them directly to mitigation strategies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion fully addresses the research question and remains consistent with the evidence (Band 3 in both strands). It meets the highest IB band for conclusions, warranting the full 3 marks.

strengths: Follows prescribed IA structure with introduction, analysis, conclusion, and appendices
Table of contents provides a clear roadmap and logical transitions
weaknesses: Table of contents formatting could be enhanced (dot leaders, consistent styling)
Research question not directly quoted in introduction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission exhibits âappropriate structure throughoutâ (Band 2). Transitions, headings, and organization all meet the top level for this criterion, yielding the full 2 marks.

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements: title page, word count, headings, citations, works cited, and appendices
Word count is clearly stated and within IB limits
weaknesses: Citation details are inconsistent (missing dates, authors) in some references
Tables and figures need better in-text discussion and consistent formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required presentation components are present and correctly formatted, meeting Band 2 standards. Despite some inconsistencies in referencing style, the criterionâs requirements are fully satisfied, meriting 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows the prescribed structure: introduction, analytical sections, conclusion, citations and appendices, all cross-referenced in a table of contents.  Transitions are logical, so the work meets the standard for âappropriate structure throughoutâ (top band for this strand).

All four documents are relevant (Gleaner articles, competitor website, interview) and are referenced in the analysis.  They provide adequate depth but do not include detailed financial or statistical data, limiting the range of ideas.  This corresponds to âdocuments are relevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the top band requiring extensive breadth.
The submission includes title page, word count, section headings, in-text citations, works-cited list and supporting documents, satisfying all required presentation elements.
Four supporting documents plus visual evidence are supplied, falling inside the required 3-5 range; this meets the highest expectations for the ânumber of documentsâ strand.

Each tool is applied directly to KingAlarmâs situation and linked to the research question; however much of the application remains qualitative and at times descriptive (e.g., listing SWOT factors without weighting).  Relevance is clear but the depth of application is uneven, fitting the descriptor âadequately applied; relevance not always clearâ at band 3.

The student incorporates some primary interview data and qualitative market information, and uses them within each tool.  Selection and use are adequate, but the evidence base is small and largely narrative; no financial projections or industry statistics are analysed.  Thus the work meets but does not exceed the âadequateâ band.

Analysis goes beyond description: comparisons with competitors, weighing of driving vs restraining forces, and evaluation of strengths/weaknesses show mostly effective reasoning.  Integration is evident across sections, though not fully sustained.  This matches the descriptor of âmostly effective analysis with some integration.â

The research questionâwhether KingAlarm should integrate ambulance servicesâis explicitly answered in the conclusion with a clear âyes, proceed with mitigationâ.  This direct answer aligns with the highest band available.

The student selects three core strategic-analysis tools (Ansoff, SWOT, Force-field) that are well suited to a diversification decision and recognised in the syllabus.  The mix shows an effective, not merely adequate, selection.

The conclusion restates the evidence from all tools and remains congruent with the SWOT and force-field outcomes, showing logical consistency.  No contradictory claims are introduced, fulfilling the highest band for consistency (3 marks).

The organisationâKingAlarm/AmbuKingâis consistently foregrounded.  Each tool (Ansoff positioning, SWOT elements, Force-field scoring) is applied directly to KingAlarmâs resources, reputation, costs and strategic aims, and primary data from an interview are incorporated.  While the connection is strong, some assertions remain unsubstantiated by quantitative evidence, so the integration is not absolutely comprehensive.  This places the work solidly in the âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ band.

The student clearly identifies the key concept of change in the title, introduction, analytical tools (Ansoff, SWOT, Force-field) and conclusion. Throughout the paper the discussion repeatedly returns to how diversification will âchangeâ KingAlarmâs operations, brand and risk profile, demonstrating a sound conceptual grasp.  However, the analysis does not weave the concept into every paragraphâseveral descriptive sections (e.g., generic definitions of tools) make little or no conceptual referenceâso the integration is partial rather than fully embedded.  This aligns best with the band that describes âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysis.â

annotations.comment: The student describes the Ansoff framework but does not position KingAlarm explicitly within the matrix. Plot the diversification cell to clarify strategic implications.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The citation of Peterdy lacks a full reference (date, URL). Ensure all inâtext references are matched in the works cited.
Word count is clearly stated and within the IB limit, demonstrating attention to presentation requirements.
Table of contents provides a clear roadmap of sections and page numbers, aiding navigation and reflecting strong structure.
Consider adding dot leaders or consistent formatting in the table of contents to enhance readability and professionalism.

The student clearly identifies âChangeâ as the key concept and references it in the introduction, establishing a conceptual focus. Consider weaving this concept explicitly into analytical sections to strengthen integration throughout.
The title is descriptive but somewhat unwieldy. A more concise phrasing of the research question would improve readability and focus.
The introduction effectively sets context with crime statistics, demonstrating awareness of the security marketâs urgency in Jamaica.
The citation for worldpopulationreview.com lacks a date and full reference. Provide complete bibliographic details to meet citation standards.
The research aim is stated but the question itself is not directly quoted. Explicitly restate the research question in a clear sentence to guide the reader.
This paragraph is descriptive but doesnât explicitly tie the delay factors back to the concept of change. Make clear how these barriers affect the planned change.
Selection of the Ansoff Matrix is appropriate for evaluating diversification, demonstrating an effective use of strategic tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The opening of the main body incorporates primary interview data effectively, showcasing adequate use of supporting documents.
Figure 1 has a caption but is not explicitly discussed in the narrative. Refer to it in the text and analyze its relevance.
The SWOT matrix is an effective choice and is correctly applied to KingAlarmâs context.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Explicitly interpret each SWOT element in terms of the change concept to deepen conceptual integration.
Market evaluation effectively highlights demand issues using Document 3; this supports the feasibility analysis.
Analysis is qualitative; consider adding market size figures or growth projections to quantify demand and strengthen evaluation.
Link the discussion of competitor loyalty more directly to barriers to change, illustrating the challenge of market entry.
SWOT factors are listed without weighting or prioritization. Assign scores or relative importance to provide a more rigorous evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Narrative discussion adds depth beyond the matrix, linking company assets to the diversification analysis.
Link each force explicitly back to how it affects organizational change to reinforce conceptual integration.
Table spans multiple sections and repeats headings, which can confuse readers. Consolidate into one formatted table.
Threats are noted but lack a critical assessment of their likelihood and impact. Evaluate which threats present the greatest risk.
Force Field Analysis is well chosen to weigh driving and restraining forces before making a change decision.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The scoring scale for driving and restraining forces is not defined. Clarify how scores (e.g., 3,4) were assigned to enhance credibility.
The conclusion draws directly on the tool analyses and answers the research question, maintaining logical consistency.
New strategies (subscription upsell) appear in the conclusion without prior analysis. Ensure all recommendations are supported by earlier sections.
Reinforce the concept of change in the conclusion by articulating how diversification embodies transformational change.
Works Cited includes a broad range of sources, demonstrating thorough research support.
Referencing style is inconsistent; some entries lack dates or author details. Adopt a uniform citation format throughout.
Some URLs are broken across lines and may be unusable. Ensure hyperlinks remain intact for verification.
The Supporting Documents section is well organized, listing each document in sequence.
Document 1 is included but lacks a brief commentary linking key evidence to the main analysis. Add annotations to show its relevance.
Consider numbering each appendix page to maintain clarity when referencing documents in the text.
